title: Secondary fronts of WWI
topic_path: Other fronts of World War I

assessment_items.question_content:
What best describes the Italian Front of World War I?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This front was pretty much a stalemate until Germany began to assist the Austrians in 1917.  They were able to penetrate Italy as far as the Piave River (approaching Venice and other major cities), but the Italians were able to push the Austrians out near the end of the War in 1918.

The Italian Front did divert resources that the Central Powers may have applied to the Western Front, but it did not play a major role in the eventual Allied victory.

choices.content: Italy was able to capture significant Austrian territory from 1915-1917
Germany was able to occupy Rome
Austrians were able to penetrate Italy with German help later in the War, but were never able to knock the Italians out
It played a major role in allowing the Allies to win World War I
Italy was able to secure territory from France

assessment_items.question_content:
Which country or empire fought on the side of the Entente in World War I?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Switzerland remained neutral throughout World War I.
The Ottoman Empire and Bulgaria both joined the Central Powers (Germany and Austria) during the war.

Prior to the war, Italy was part of the Triple Alliance with Germany and Austria.  However, when it did enter the War in 1915, it joined on the side of the Entente mainly due to promised territory and historical animosity with the Austrians.

This is detailed [in this video](/humanities/history/euro-hist/other-fronts-ww1/v/italy-backs-out-of-triple-alliance).

choices.content: the Ottoman Empire
the Austro-Hungarian Empire
Bulgaria
Italy
Switzerland

assessment_items.question_content:
What is **NOT** an accurate description of Italy immediately before and during World War I?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is covered in [this video](/humanities/history/euro-hist/other-fronts-ww1/v/italy-backs-out-of-triple-alliance).

Italy and Austria had an uncomfortable relationship within the Triple Alliance since Austria had been a historic enemy of Italian unification and it had territory that Italy thought should be part of Italy.

Partially motivated to regain some of that territory, Italy entered World War I in 1915 on the side of the Triple Entente against Austria.
Italy *did not* declare war on France to avenge French seizure of Tunisia.

choices.content: Italy was formally a member of the Triple Alliance with Germany and Austria before the War
Italy declared war on France to avenge French seizure of Tunisia
Italy was a historic enemy of Austria  (which had been an enemy of Italian unification in the late 1800s)
Italy was eager to lay claim on territory that was being held by the Austrians
Italy formally joins the Triple Entente in 1915

assessment_items.question_content:
Which statement is **not** true regarding Greek involvement in World War I?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is covered in [this video](/humanities/history/euro-hist/other-fronts-ww1/v/serbian-and-macedonian-fronts).

After the Allied Blockade helps overthrow the King in 1917, Greece officially enters the War on the side of the Allies, not the Central Powers.

choices.content: Greece was officially neutral from 1914 until 1917
King Constantine I leaned towards favoring the Central Powers
Prime Minister Venizelos favored the Allies
Greece eventually enters the War on the side of the Central Powers
The Allied Powers blockade Greece in 1917 which forces the King into exile

assessment_items.question_content:
Roughly what percentage of the Serbian military was killed during World War I?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Entering the war, Serbia had a 400,000-man army.  Roughly 60% of them died during the war.

assessment_items.question_content:
Which of the following is **not** true about Serbia during World War I?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is covered in [this video](/humanities/history/euro-hist/other-fronts-ww1/v/serbian-and-macedonian-fronts).

The Allies were able to mount an offensive against the Central Powers in Serbia in Sept of 1918 to retake Serbia (which was two months before the armistice that ended World War I).

choices.content: The Serbians were able to hold back the initial Austrian invasion in 1914
The Central Powers were able to successfully invade and occupy Serbia in 1915
The Serbian Army was badly equipped relative to the Austrians
Some Serbian forces were able to escape the Central Power invasion and regroup with other Allied forces in Salonica
The Central Powers were able to control Serbia until the end of World War I in November 1918

assessment_items.question_content:
What best describes Japan's role in World War I?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The question is covered in [this video](/humanities/history/euro-hist/other-fronts-ww1/v/japan-in-world-war-i).

In 1914, Japan was eager to increase its influence in China and the Pacific.  It was already on good terms with the United Kingdom and, in August 1914, declared war on Germany (and then Austria-Hungary).  It soon took possession of Germany's colonies in Asia and the Pacific.

It was in World War II that Japan was allied with the Germans and provoked the United States by attacking Pearl Harbor.

choices.content: It was allied with Germany against the United States
It was the decisive reason why Russia left the war early
It attacked and took over German possessions in China and the Pacific
It provoked the U.S. into World War I by attacking Pearl Harbor
It had several conflicts with the British dominion of Australia
It remained neutral through the War
